steps.title: Identify the sentence structures
steps.content:
The first clause (“From 2009 to 2019, travelers flew out of major urban airports with increasing [noun]”) is independent.

The second clause (“Washington Dulles International Airport in Washington, DC, saw a 6.9 percent increase”) is independent.

The phrase “for example” must transition between them.

steps.title: Apply the boundary rule
steps.content:
The rule against comma splices requires that two independent clauses be separated by a period, semicolon, or coordinating conjunction.

Choice D places a period after the first clause and begins the new sentence with “For example,” to introduce the example.

Options A and C violate this rule by joining independent clauses with commas alone, while B uses improper punctuation (a comma before a semicolon).

That matches choice D — D is correct.
